title: Convert to smaller units (mm, cm, m, & km)
description: Practice converting a metric measure of length to a smaller unit.
topic_path: Convert to smaller units (mm, cm, m, & km)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert.**  

${}$ | ${}$
-: | :-: | :-
$8$ meters $21$ centimeters | $=$ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]] centimeters

[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints: We need to convert $\blueD{8}$ meters $\maroonD{21}$ centimeters to centimeters.

First, let's convert $\blueD{8}$ meters to centimeters.

$1 \text{ meter} = \greenD{100}\text{ centimeters}$


$\blueD{8} \text{ meters} = \blueD{8}\times \greenD{100}=\blueD{800}\text{ centimeters}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{21}$ centimeters.

$\blueD{800}\text{ cm}+\maroonD{21}\text{ cm}=\blueD8\maroonD{21}\text{ cm}$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
$1$ meter $= 100$ centimeters| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ kilogram $= 1{,}000$ grams|
$1$ meter $= 1{,}000$ millimeters|| ||$1$ gram $= 1{,}000$ milligrams|
$1$ centimeter $= 10$ millimeters|| |||

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert.**  

${}$ | ${}$
-: | :-: | :-
$11$ kilometers $375$ meters | $=$ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters

[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints: We need to convert $\blueD{11}$ kilometers $\maroonD{375}$ meters to meters.

First, let's convert $\blueD{11}$ kilometers to meters.

$1 \text{ kilometer} = \greenD{1{,}000}\text{ meters}$


$\blueD{11} \text{ kilometers} = \blueD{11}\times \greenD{1{,}000}=\blueD{11{,}000}\text{ meters}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{375}$ meters.

$\blueD{11{,}000}\text{ m}+\maroonD{375}\text{ m}=\blueD{11}{,}\maroonD{375}\text{ m}$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
$1$ meter $= 100$ centimeters| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ kilogram $= 1{,}000$ grams|
$1$ meter $= 1{,}000$ millimeters|| ||$1$ gram $= 1{,}000$ milligrams|
$1$ centimeter $= 10$ millimeters|| |||

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert.**  

${}$ | ${}$
-: | :-: | :-
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] millimeters |$=$| $4$ meters $85$ millimeters

[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints: We need to convert $\blueD{4}$ meters $\maroonD{85}$ millimeters to millimeters.

First, let's convert $\blueD{4}$ meters to millimeters.

$1 \text{ meter} = \greenD{1{,}000}\text{ millimeters}$


$\blueD{4} \text{ meters} = \blueD{4}\times \greenD{1{,}000}=\blueD{4{,}000}\text{ millimeters}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{85}$ millimeters.

$\blueD{4{,}000}\text{ mm}+\maroonD{85}\text{ mm}=\blueD{4}{,}\blueD0\maroonD{85}\text{ mm}$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
$1$ meter $= 100$ centimeters| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ kilogram $= 1{,}000$ grams|
$1$ meter $= 1{,}000$ millimeters|| ||$1$ gram $= 1{,}000$ milligrams|
$1$ centimeter $= 10$ millimeters|| |||

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert.**  

${}$ | ${}$
-: | :-: | :-
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] millimeters |$=$| $15$ centimeters $4$ millimeters

[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints: We need to convert $\blueD{15}$ centimeters $\maroonD{4}$ millimeters to millimeters.

First, let's convert $\blueD{15}$ centimeters to millimeters.

$1 \text{ centimeter} = \greenD{10}\text{ millimeters}$


$\blueD{15} \text{ centimeters} = \blueD{15}\times \greenD{10}=\blueD{150}\text{ millimeters}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{4}$ millimeters.

$\blueD{150}\text{ mm}+\maroonD{4}\text{ mm}=\blueD{15}\maroonD{4}\text{ mm}$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
$1$ meter $= 100$ centimeters| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ kilogram $= 1{,}000$ grams|
$1$ meter $= 1{,}000$ millimeters|| ||$1$ gram $= 1{,}000$ milligrams|
$1$ centimeter $= 10$ millimeters|| |||

assessment_items.question_content:
**Compare using $>,<,$ or $=$.**

$65$  millimeters [[☃ dropdown 1]] $5$ centimeters 

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
To compare, we need the numbers to have the same units.  

Let's convert $\blueD5$ centimeters to millimeters.

Now, we can compare.

$65\text{ millimeters} > \blueD{50}\text{ millimeters}$
